section.heading: Contempt authority; assistance to the Court

subsection.chapeau: The Court shall have power to punish by fine or imprisonment such contempt of its authority as—

paragraph.content: misbehavior of any person in its presence or so near thereto as to obstruct the administration of justice;
misbehavior of any of its officers in their official transactions; or
disobedience or resistance to its lawful writ, process, order, rule, decree, or command.

subsection.content: The Court shall have such assistance in the carrying out of its lawful writ, process, order, rule, decree, or command as is available to a court of the United States. The United States marshal for a district in which the Court is sitting shall, if requested by the chief judge of the Court, attend any session of the Court in that district.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 100–687, div. A, title III, § 301(a), Nov. 18, 1988, 102 Stat. 4116, § 4065; renumbered § 7265, Pub. L. 102–40, title IV, § 402(b)(1), May 7, 1991, 105 Stat. 238.)

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1991—Pub. L. 102–40 renumbered section 4065 of this title as this section.
